final_answer:
No, one real scope-relevant gap remains.

Why this matters for Gold Chain: the new snapshot-eligibility path can still deadlock approvals if one operator controls multiple snapshot validators, because voter resolution is operator-based and ambiguous.

Confirmed facts from read-only audit:
1. The intended fix is implemented: pending nonce votes now resolve from snapshot, and live-set gating is only on new proposal start in [validator_lifecycle.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator_lifecycle.go#L202).
2. The new helper [snapshotVoterValIDForOperator](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator_lifecycle.go#L708) errors on multiple snapshot matches for one operator.
3. No operator-uniqueness invariant exists in stake module (no guard found).
4. New regression tests pass, including drift cases in [msg_server_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/msg_server_test.go#L195) and full `x/stake` tests pass.

Current blocker:
- If one operator maps to multiple snapshot validators, vote attribution is not uniquely representable, so required snapshot power can become unreachable.

What should be done next:
1. Choose one explicit rule: either enforce operator uniqueness chain-wide, or support multi-validator operators with explicit per-validator voter identity in approval votes.
2. Implement that rule at proposal creation (reject invalid snapshot upfront) so rounds cannot start in an unfinalizable state.
3. Add a regression test for “same operator, multiple snapshot validators” and rerun `go test ./x/stake/... -count=1` from `bridge/gilt-consensus`.
prompt: Read only task, don't change code. Go through fine tooth comb of what you just implemented and check for gaps or partial implementation and report back please. Dont be overzealous and go on tangents, remain in the confines of the scope of this task, there might be times where you might have to say all done nothing left, which would also be an acceptable response
